taskDescription: Click on the key embryological terms in the text below.
textField:
Human development begins with *fertilisation* in the ampulla of the uterine tube. The *zygote* undergoes rapid mitotic divisions called *cleavage* to form a solid ball of cells known as the *morula*. As fluid accumulates, the *blastocyst* forms with an inner cell mass (*embryoblast*) and an outer layer (*trophoblast*).

*Implantation* occurs around day 6-7 when the blastocyst embeds in the *endometrium*. During the second week, the embryoblast differentiates into two layers: *epiblast* and *hypoblast*, forming the bilaminar disc. The first two weeks constitute the *all-or-none* period of teratogen susceptibility.

*Gastrulation* begins in the third week with formation of the *primitive* *streak*. Cells migrate through the streak to form the three *germ* layers: *ectoderm*, *mesoderm*, and *endoderm*. The *notochord* induces the overlying ectoderm to form the *neural* plate, initiating *neurulation*. The embryonic period (weeks 3-8) is the critical period for *organogenesis* and major *congenital* malformations.

overallFeedback.feedback: Good effort! Some key terms were missed.
Excellent! You identified the key embryological terms.
